---
title: "Project 1 Checkpoint 3"
format: html
---

Description of csv: 

Child immunization, measles, measures the percentage of children ages 12-23 months who received the measles vaccination before 12 months or at any time before the survey. A child is considered adequately immunized against measles after receiving one dose of vaccine.

```{r}

#Reading in Child Immunization of measles by country csv file 
#data cleaning to start counting immunization rate in 1985 and end in 2024 (where majority of countries had actual data, and not NA)

raw_child_vacc <- read.csv("ChildImmunizationMeasles.csv", skip = 4, header = TRUE, fill = TRUE, blank.lines.skip = TRUE)

vars <- names(raw_child_vacc) 
yrs <- which(substr(vars, 1, 1) == "X")
vars[yrs] <- substr(vars[yrs], 2, nchar(vars[yrs]))
names(raw_child_vacc) <- vars 

head(raw_child_vacc)
#cleaned data from csv 
#USE THIS 
child_vacc <- raw_child_vacc |>
  select(Country.Name, Country.Code, `1985` : `2024`) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = `1985`:`2024`,
               names_to = "Year",
               values_to = "VaccinationRate") |>
  mutate(Year = as.integer(Year))

head(child_vacc)
```
